info.title: OpenClaw Orchestrator API
info.description:
Release-quality route contract for the current orchestrator-owned control plane.

Runtime cutover truth:
- `/operator` and `/operator/*` are served by orchestrator from the built `operator-s-console` bundle.
- Public proof remains on orchestrator-owned public routes, not on the retired `openclawdbot` lane.

Security and browser contract:
- Protected routes require `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
- Browser CORS is backend-owned and deny-by-default via explicit origin allowlists.
- Protected routes apply a pre-auth abuse guard (`300 / 60s / IP`) before role-aware limiter buckets.

Rate-limit buckets:
- viewer-read: `120 / 60s` per actor/key label
- operator-write: `30 / 60s` per actor/key label
- admin-export: `10 / 60s` per actor/key label

This document describes the orchestrator JSON route contract; it does not model the `/operator` SPA shell itself as an API surface.

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: Task catalog, queueing, and run-ledger surfaces.

tags.name: Incidents
tags.description: Runtime incident ledger and remediation surfaces.

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Per-agent readiness, capability, and topology surfaces.

tags.name: Governance
tags.description: Governed skill registry, policy, telemetry, and audit surfaces.

tags.name: Knowledge
tags.description: Knowledge summary, query, and export surfaces.

tags.name: Persistence
tags.description: Persistence health, summary, historical, and export surfaces.

tags.name: Proof
tags.description: Curated public proof surfaces served by orchestrator.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Protected auth and RBAC context surfaces.

bearerAuth.description: Protected routes require `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Auth context is established before role enforcement.

webhookSignature.name: X-Webhook-Signature
webhookSignature.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature over the canonicalized JSON request body. Prefix `sha256=` is accepted but optional.

X-Request-Id.description: Generated request correlation id for protected routes.

X-API-Key-Expires.description: Expiry timestamp for the authenticated API key on protected routes.

Retry-After.description: Minimum number of seconds a client should wait before retrying.

ratelimit-limit.description: Current route bucket request limit for the active window.

ratelimit-remaining.description: Remaining requests in the active limiter window.

ratelimit-reset.description: Seconds until the current limiter window resets.

X-OpenClaw-Cache.description: Response cache result for cached read surfaces.

X-OpenClaw-Cache-Store.description: Backing store used for the cached response.

Cache-Control.description: Server-selected cache-control policy for the response.

Vary.description: Protected cached reads vary on Authorization and, where relevant, Origin or preflight headers.

RunId.name: runId
RunId.description: Task run identity. Runtime uses an explicit idempotency key when provided; otherwise the task id becomes the run id.

ApprovalId.name: id
ApprovalId.description: Approval task id from the approval ledger.

TaskRunType.name: type
TaskRunType.description: Optional task type filter for task runs.

TaskRunStatus.name: status
TaskRunStatus.description: Optional execution status filter for task runs.

IncludeInternal.name: includeInternal
IncludeInternal.description: When true, internal maintenance runs such as heartbeat and startup remain in the ledger response.

IncludeResolved.name: includeResolved
IncludeResolved.description: When true, resolved incidents remain in the list response.

DeniedOnly.name: deniedOnly
DeniedOnly.description: When true, only denied skill/tool invocations are returned.

MemoryAgentId.name: agentId
MemoryAgentId.description: Optional single-agent filter for memory recall.

MemoryIncludeErrors.name: includeErrors
MemoryIncludeErrors.description: When false, error timeline entries are omitted.

MemoryIncludeSensitive.name: includeSensitive
MemoryIncludeSensitive.description: When true, default memory redaction is relaxed for the response.

PersistenceDays.name: days
PersistenceDays.description: Historical persistence lookback window in days.

createdAt.description: Queue task creation timestamp. Runtime currently returns the enqueue timestamp as a number-like value.

PersistenceHistoricalResponse.description: Historical persistence payload from the persistence subsystem. Shape depends on the requested aggregation.

BadRequest.description: Request validation or route-level input parsing failed.

Unauthorized.description: Bearer token missing, invalid, or expired, or webhook signature missing/invalid.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated actor does not satisfy the route role requirement.

Conflict.description: Request conflicts with current governed runtime state.

get.description: Shallow public liveness check. This is not the authoritative operator-health contract; use `/api/health/extended` for protected operator health.

x-openclaw-access.shellContract: Orchestrator serves `/operator` and `/operator/*` via the built operator-s-console bundle; this route remains public JSON only.

200.description: Public health payload with helper URLs.

get.description: Returns the orchestrator-owned OpenAPI document for the current runtime route surface.
Read-only knowledge summary with runtime freshness, provenance, and contradiction signals.
Lightweight public health probe for the persistence dependency layer.
Curated orchestrator-owned public proof overview. This is public proof, not protected control-plane state.
Curated public control-lane proof payload sourced from orchestrator runtime state.
Curated public demand summary sourced from live orchestrator demand state.
Live public demand snapshot sourced from current orchestrator runtime state.
Curated latest milestone proof feed.
Dead-letter or blocked proof-delivery items kept on the public proof side as a curated risk surface.

get.summary: Resolved auth actor and role context
get.description: Protected identity surface. Auth executes before role enforcement and returns the resolved actor, highest role, role set, request id, and API key metadata.
Protected task catalog that combines operator-facing task profiles with current operational metadata.
Bounded read-only companion view over current control-plane mode, primary operator move, pressure story, and proof posture.
Bounded read-only companion view over the operator task catalog with readiness, approval posture, and dependency caveats.
Bounded read-only companion view over incident classification pressure, acknowledgement posture, remediation posture, and the ranked queue.
Bounded read-only companion view over recent run posture, operator summaries, next actions, and freshness/watch cues.
Bounded read-only companion view over dominant pending approval lanes, oldest waiting posture, and top approval items.

post.description: Protected task enqueue endpoint. Runtime injects `__actor`, `__role`, and `__requestId` before central admission. A reused idempotency key is reported as duplicate-suppressed and never emits accepted/queued telemetry.

200.description: Duplicate task suppressed before queue admission.

get.description: Protected run ledger with workflow, approval, proof-link, and repair metadata. Internal maintenance runs are hidden by default unless `includeInternal=true`.
Protected task run detail by stable run id (explicit idempotency key or task id fallback).
Protected approval inbox for approval-gated tasks and review-gated replay paths.

post.description: Protected approval decision route. An approved decision queues a replay task with `approvedFromTaskId` set.

get.description: Protected incident list over the runtime-generated incident ledger with status/classification filtering.
Protected incident detail by stable incident id.
Protected incident history stream with acknowledgements, ownership history, and remediation task records.

post.description: Protected incident acknowledgement route for operator action tracking.
Protected incident ownership route for manual operator assignment.
Protected remediation entrypoint. Creates a bounded remediation task record linked to the incident and queues the actual task when allowed by policy.

get.description: Protected operator aggregation for queue, approvals, governance, self-healing, truth layers, topology, incidents, and recent tasks. This is not the authoritative system-health route.
Protected control-plane health contract with control-plane, worker, repair, dependency, truth-layer, topology, and incident splits.

get.summary: Runtime facts and effective control-plane configuration
get.description: Protected runtime facts surface with effective state-store target, scheduler truth, internal/public task exposure, and resident-worker inventory.
Protected agent readiness surface with declaration, worker/service truth, capability, topology, and relationship-history views.
Protected governed-skill registry with trust posture, executability, persistence mode, executor binding, and provenance snapshot.
Protected governed-skill policy summary derived from current orchestrator governance state.
Protected ToolGate invocation summary over governed skill traffic.
Protected paginated ToolGate/governed-skill audit stream. `deniedOnly=true` filters to denied invocations.
Protected memory recall across persisted per-agent memory state. Responses are redacted by default unless `includeSensitive=true` is set.

post.description: Protected knowledge query with request-body validation, knowledge index results, and runtime freshness/provenance signals.

get.description: Admin-only knowledge export. Runtime returns markdown by default and JSON when `format=json` is requested.
Protected historical persistence data. Shape varies by persistence implementation and requested aggregation.
Protected persistence summary with storage, collection, retention, and indicator slices.
Admin-only export of persistence data from the current runtime backend.

post.description: Signed AlertManager-style webhook. Uses HMAC verification rather than bearer authentication.
